children.heading: .—Provision of assistance
children.label: 1 .—Provision of assistance
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations and for the purpose of mitigating the impacts of Coast Guard actions including expansion of bases, including direct or indirect impacts, to natural resources and cultural resources, the Commandant may provide Federal financial assistance, except for loans or loan guarantees, or make grants to an eligible entity.

children.label: A
children.text: limit any development or use of such natural resources and cultural resources as a result of such Coast Guard actions described in paragraph (1);

children.label: i
children.text: Tribal treaty fisheries and shellfish harvest, and usual and accustomed fishing areas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsistence fisheries, or any other fishery or shellfish harvest, of an Indian Tribe;

children.label: C
children.text: provide a means to replace, repair, or restore such natural resources and cultural resources of an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization if such property is damaged by Coast Guard actions described in paragraph (1), in consultation with the affected Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization; and

children.label: D
children.text: maintain and improve natural resources located outside a Coast Guard installation, if the purpose of the agreement is to relieve or eliminate current or anticipated challenges that could restrict, impede, or otherwise interfere with, directly or indirectly, current or anticipated Coast Guard actions described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: Financial assistance or grants made under paragraph (1) may not be used for the purpose of the Coast Guard receiving any funds.

children.heading: Notification; Availability of Agreements to Congress.—
children.label: b Notification; Availability of Agreements to Congress.—

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: 1 .—Notification
children.text: The Commandant shall notify the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation or the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate (and the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate in the case of an eligible entity that is an Indian Tribe, Tribal organization or Native Hawaiian organization) and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives in writing not later than the date that is 3 full business days prior to any day on which the Commandant intends to enter into such an agreement or contract, or make a grant under subsection (a), and include in such notification the anticipated costs of carrying out the agreement, to the extent practicable.

children.heading: .—Availability of agreements
children.label: 2 .—Availability of agreements
children.text: A copy of such financial assistance or grant made under subsection (a) shall be provided to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation or the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate or the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives not later than 5 full business days after the date on which such request is submitted to the Commandant.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: c .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to undermine the rights of any Indian Tribe to seek full and meaningful government-to-government consultation under this section or under any other law.

children.label: A
children.text: A building, structure, site, district, or object eligible for or included in the National Register of Historic Places maintained under .section 302101 of title 54

children.label: B
children.text: Cultural items, as such term is defined in section 2(3) of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ().25 U.S.C. 3001(3)

children.label: C
children.text: An archaeological resource, as such term is defined in section 3(1) of the Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 ()).16 U.S.C. 470bb(1)

children.label: D
children.text: An archaeological artifact collection and associated records covered by part 79 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.label: E
children.text: A sacred site, as such term is defined in section 1(b) of Executive Order No. 13007 ( note; relating to Indian sacred sites).42 U.S.C. 1996

children.label: F
children.text: Treaty or trust resources of an Indian Tribe, including the habitat associated with such resources.

children.label: G
children.text: Subsistence resources of an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization including the habitat associated with such resources.

children.label: A
children.text: A State, or a political subdivision of a State.

children.label: B
children.text: A local government.

children.label: C
children.text: An Indian Tribe.

children.label: D
children.text: Native Hawaiian organization.

children.label: E
children.text: A Tribal organization.

children.heading: .—Indian tribe
children.label: 3 .—Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given that term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 5304

children.heading: .—Native hawaiian organization
children.label: 4 .—Native hawaiian organization
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian organization” has the meaning given such term in section 6207 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 () except the term includes the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands and the Office of Hawaiian Affairs.20 U.S.C. 7517

children.label: A
children.text: Surface water resources.

children.label: B
children.text: Ground water resources.

children.label: C
children.text: Air resources.

children.label: D
children.text: Geologic resources.

children.label: E
children.text: Biological resources.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 6 .—State
children.text: The term “State” includes each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the territories and possessions of the United States.

children.heading: .—Tribal organization
children.label: 7 .—Tribal organization
children.text: The term “Tribal organization” has the meaning given such term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 5304
